user_story: As an Edge Node user, I can start Dispatch from my SQL directory and keep that launch context.
expected_behavior: App captures Path.cwd once, shows the short CWD in the title area, opens Dashboard, and refreshes Kerberos on a timer.
manual_test: cd to SQL directory and run dispatch; New Job should use that directory.
retest_status: Local automated pass; Edge CWD check pending

user_story: As a keyboard user, I can reach core destinations and maintenance actions globally.
expected_behavior: Global bindings support q, ?, F2; command palette exposes Overview, New Job, History, Browse metadata, and kinit.
manual_test: Press ?, F2, and command palette in a real terminal.
local_test_status: Automated covered; focused F2 sidebar test passed
errors_found: Ctrl+B is a poor tmux/SSH shortcut and the old action queried the wrong scope
fix_status: Changed sidebar shortcut to F2 and toggle action to query the active screen
retest_status: Focused local retest passed; SSH key delivery pending

user_story: As a user on a small SSH terminal, I get a warning instead of silent broken layout.
expected_behavior: The app warns below 80x24 and keeps layouts usable at 80x24.
manual_test: Resize below 80x24 over SSH.
local_test_status: Automated 80x24 render and below-min notification covered
retest_status: Focused local retest passed; real terminal resize pending

user_story: As an operator, I can diagnose startup and install-version drift from local evidence.
expected_behavior: App startup configures ~/.dispatch/dispatch.log, writes a startup entry, and warns when installed_version is missing or differs from the running package version.
manual_test: Start Dispatch with an intentionally stale installed_version and inspect ~/.dispatch/dispatch.log.
local_test_status: Automated startup log and version-warning coverage

user_story: As a user, I can navigate consistently from top-level and nested screens.
expected_behavior: Sidebar items route through the app, flatten stale top-level stack state, and require a selected job for View Logs.
manual_test: Click sidebar entries from New Job, History, Browser, and Job Detail.

user_story: As a user on a narrow terminal, I can collapse navigation while preserving status.
expected_behavior: Sidebar auto-collapses below width 100 or via F2; icons, active item, and Kerberos chip remain visible.
manual_test: Resize below width 100 and press F2.
local_test_status: Automated collapse, manual override, icon, and Kerberos chip coverage
errors_found: Sidebar toggle action did not find screen-mounted sidebars; Ctrl+B conflicted with tmux usage
fix_status: Fixed active-screen query, manual override, and changed shortcut to F2
retest_status: Focused local retest passed; SSH visual check pending

user_story: As a supervisor, I can see current and recent jobs with running jobs prioritized.
expected_behavior: Dashboard refreshes active jobs off-thread, pins Running first, caps visible rows at 100, and shows id/source/destination/state/elapsed.
manual_test: Seed Running and recent terminal manifests; verify ordering.

user_story: As a user, I can understand capacity and Kerberos readiness at a glance.
expected_behavior: Status strip shows RUNNING n/2, finished/failed 7D counts, and Kerberos missing/low/healthy TTL.
manual_test: Verify status strip with healthy and missing ticket over SSH.
local_test_status: Automated covered including missing/low/healthy Kerberos matrix
retest_status: Local automated pass; real klist pending

user_story: As a user with many jobs, I can filter the cockpit without per-keystroke filesystem work.
expected_behavior: Slash opens filter; cached jobs are filtered by id/source/destination/state; Escape clears and refocuses the table.
manual_test: Press /, type a state or id fragment, then Escape.

user_story: As a supervisor, I can preview the highlighted job log without opening detail.
expected_behavior: Dashboard selects a running or first job, tails a small log window, updates on row highlight, and hides the pane on short terminals.
manual_test: Highlight multiple jobs; verify log preview follows.

user_story: As a keyboard user, I can launch, view, cancel, and navigate from the cockpit.
expected_behavior: N opens New Job, V opens selected Job Detail, C opens selected Job Detail with cancel flow, H opens History, B opens Browser, Enter opens detail.
manual_test: Press each binding with seeded rows.

user_story: As a new or cleaned-up user, I get a useful empty cockpit instead of a blank table.
expected_behavior: When there are no recent jobs, the dashboard shows the current empty-state copy and the bottom event trail records startup with a timestamp.
manual_test: Start Dispatch with no jobs and inspect the empty state plus event trail.

user_story: As a user, I can review jobs older than the dashboard window.
expected_behavior: History loads old terminal jobs off-thread and displays id, target, state, and finished time.
manual_test: Seed old finished manifests; press H.

user_story: As a user with many historical jobs, I can page, sort, search, and open details.
expected_behavior: Search filters cached jobs; page size is 17; [ and ] page; S cycles sort; Enter/View Logs opens full row-key job id.
manual_test: Use search, paging, sorting, and Enter on real history.

user_story: As a user, I can discover keyboard actions without leaving the TUI.
expected_behavior: ? opens modal help; Esc or ? dismisses; help lists global and screen-specific bindings.
manual_test: Press ? and close it over SSH.

user_story: As a user, I can open New Job from the captured launch directory with sensible defaults.
expected_behavior: New Job receives launch_cwd; default SQL is first *.sql or query.sql; dates default to current month; email seeds from DISPATCH_EMAIL.

user_story: As a user, I can see the legal Source x Destination cells.

user_story: As a user, I cannot choose unsupported source/destination pairs.

user_story: As a user, I can pick a SQL file from the launch directory instead of typing a path.

user_story: As a user, template SQL is recognized automatically.

user_story: As a user, I get immediate feedback before launch.

user_story: As a user, I can run kinit from New Job and refresh ticket status.

user_story: As a user, invalid launches are refused before any manifest or runner starts.

user_story: As a user, I can preview what will run before launching.

user_story: As a user, I must explicitly confirm before a job starts.
expected_behavior: Launch validates first, opens a danger ConfirmScreen with source/destination/table/CSV/email, and cancel returns without runner launch.

expected_behavior: Job dir, job.sql, manifest, orchestrator calls, and detached runner launch are created only after confirmation; manifest writes tolerate transient Windows replace locks.

errors_found: Windows occasionally raised PermissionError replacing manifest.tmp with manifest.json during runner completion
fix_status: Added retry/backoff around atomic manifest replace

user_story: As a repeat user or smoke harness, I can reuse common form values.

user_story: As a user, I can review generated SQL before launch.

user_story: As a user, I can inspect a job summary and live logs.

retest_status: Focused and full local validation passed; Edge CSV smoke pending

user_story: As a user, job states and logs are clear.

user_story: As a user, cancel stops running work and marks it cancelled.

user_story: As a user, I cannot exceed two simultaneous Running jobs.

user_story: As a user, one install command creates my runtime and launcher.

user_story: As a repeat user, common launch fields are remembered.

expected_behavior: Production policy must block non-smoke drops while allowing cleanup of dispatch_smoke_* resources created by the current smoke run.

user_story: As a tester, I need Python validation commands to match the detected Edge Node Python version.

manual_test: Run PYTHON_BIN commands on Edge Node.
local_test_status: Documentation and harness command fixed locally
errors_found: Python version hardcoding mismatch and automated smoke install omitted DISPATCH_PYTHON_BIN
fix_status: Fixed Level 1 docs and automated smoke install command to use DISPATCH_PYTHON_BIN
retest_status: Full local validation passed; Edge command pending

user_story: As a maintainer, I need historical audit pass counts to read as historical, not current suite status.
expected_behavior: Old UI/UX audit should label 120 passed as audit-time evidence because the current suite has grown.
manual_test: Read audit header and validation table.

user_story: As a tester, I can ask the production harness for top-level help before starting an authenticated Edge session.
expected_behavior: py -m tools.prod_tui --help prints usage, lists tmux/smoke/job/level commands, and exits successfully.
manual_test: Run py -m tools.prod_tui --help locally.
local_test_status: Focused CLI help regression and full local suite passed
errors_found: Top-level --help returned Unknown command with exit code 1
fix_status: Added shared usage printer and -h/--help handling

user_story: As a tester, I get reliable local tmux startup and an actionable harness start failure instead of a Python traceback when SSH does not reach a prompt.
expected_behavior: py -m tools.prod_tui tmux start --config tools/prod_tui/config.yaml opens a local shell first for psmux, sends a Windows-safe SSH command, catches timeout/auth/session-start failures, cleans up stale sessions, prints a concise error to stderr, and exits nonzero.
manual_test: Run py -m tools.prod_tui tmux start --config tools/prod_tui/config.yaml without a live Edge prompt.
local_test_status: Focused psmux startup, quoting, and start-failure regressions passed
errors_found: Harness start printed a raw traceback, used direct-command psmux startup that showed a blank pane, used Unix single-quote SSH remote command quoting on Windows, and left stale local sessions after failure
fix_status: Start local shell first, send SSH into it, use Windows-safe command quoting, catch failures, and stop stale sessions
retest_status: Full local validation passed; Edge SSH network/auth prompt pending

user_story: As a tester, I need the production harness README to match the current module CLI, Python contract, and single authenticated-pane model.
expected_behavior: README should document python -m tools.prod_tui commands, python3.11/python3.10 support, and one-off remote commands reusing the already-authenticated tmux pane.
manual_test: Read README prerequisites, Manual tmux CLI, smoke, job, and deploy sections.
errors_found: README still described separate SSH for one-off remote commands, older script entrypoints, and python3.10-only prerequisites
fix_status: Updated README to module CLI, python3.11/python3.10 support, and pane-reused auth model

user_story: As a tester, I can map manual CSV smoke and Table+Csv validation to the executable harness level.
expected_behavior: Production docs should say Level 3a is manual CSV smoke and Level 4 is executable job-type breadth for Csv and Table+Csv.
manual_test: Read production testing levels and run py -m tools.prod_tui level --config tools/prod_tui/config.yaml --level 4 on Edge.
errors_found: Level 3a manual CSV checklist was not tied clearly to executable Level 4 harness coverage
fix_status: Updated production docs to map Csv/Table+Csv to Level 4
retest_status: Full local validation passed; Level 4 Edge run pending

user_story: As a contributor, I understand why the corporate Bitbucket remote URL differs from the GitHub repo name.
expected_behavior: Development workflow should document that the current bitbucket URL ending in autobench.git is the configured Edge-pull remote for this working tree unless the owner changes it.
manual_test: Review Remotes section.
errors_found: Bitbucket remote URL looked surprising for the Robocop repo
fix_status: Documented the naming artifact and owner-change boundary

user_story: As a developer, I can run the documented local check even when C: temp space is tight.
expected_behavior: tools/dev/local_check.ps1 should run pytest with repo-local TEMP/TMP and --basetemp, then clean those temp directories; .gitignore should ignore interrupted temp folders.
manual_test: Run .\\tools\\dev\\local_check.ps1.
local_test_status: Focused regression and actual local_check passed
errors_found: Full pytest failed when C: temporary space filled and pytest could not create tmp_path directories
fix_status: Updated local_check to use repo-local temp dirs for pytest and clean them; ignored interrupted temp dirs

user_story: As a tester, I can detect Edge network reachability before requesting RSA/Kerberos validation time.
expected_behavior: Production docs and harness README should include config-driven `python -m tools.prod_tui preflight` commands for node 03 and node 04, optional JSON evidence output, direct Test-NetConnection TCP 2222 fallback, single-authenticated-pane wording, a selected-node `--config` discipline, and an explicit stop rule when the preflight report has `connected: false`.
manual_test: Run python -m tools.prod_tui preflight --config tools/prod_tui/config.yaml --timeout 5 --json-report tools/prod_tui/reports/preflight-node03.json or the matching node 04 command before harness start.
local_test_status: Focused TCP preflight command/docs regressions passed
errors_found: Docs did not tell testers to preflight TCP 2222, production_testing still implied separate direct ssh for one-off commands, the harness had no config-driven preflight command or durable preflight report output, the harness section could still lead operators to start tmux/smoke/job/level after a failed preflight, and node-selection handoff could fall back to node 03 when node 04 was intended
fix_status: Added config-driven TCP preflight command, optional JSON report output, direct Test-NetConnection fallback, single-authenticated-pane wording, explicit stop-until-connected guidance, node 04 preflight path, and selected-node --config discipline
retest_status: Actual local_check passed; Edge TCP preflight pending because TCP 2222 currently fails
